chunks.content: Santum is a South African all-in-one online mental wellness platform that provides access to professional online therapy and self-help resources.
Santum describes its mission as helping people overcome day-to-day challenges so they can live a happier and healthier life.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks if therapists are professional
chunks.content: Santum presents its therapists as registered, verified, experienced, licensed professionals matched to users according to individual needs.
Santum combines paid therapy access with self-help courses, wellness apps, wellness audio and regular wellness content.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum is online
chunks.content: Santum is positioned as an online therapy platform, offering therapy through digital access rather than traditional in-room sessions.
Users can sign up for free membership on Santum. The public site states that free membership includes access to therapists, free apps, therapeutic audio and weekly wellness content.
To start, users complete a registration form and a short assessment questionnaire before accessing matched therapists and wellness resources.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks where to choose therapist
chunks.content: After signup, users access a private dashboard where they can review matched therapists and choose one according to experience and personal fit.
The public Santum signup flow displays an over-18 confirmation. Santum’s FAQ also says Santum is not right for minors.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks what happens after signup
chunks.content: Santum says users can take time to become comfortable in their private membership section before deciding whether to purchase counselling sessions or messaging.
Santum lists Individual Plan, Couples Plan, Teens Plan, Staff Plan and Gift Therapy as therapy pathways on the public site.
The Individual Plan is presented for users dealing with issues such as anxiety, depression or isolation.
The Couples Plan is presented for marriage or relationship stress.

The Teens Plan is presented for a child experiencing issues such as addiction or low self-esteem, but Santum’s FAQ says users should be at least 18; this should be verified before AI gives operational instructions about minors.
The Staff Plan is presented for employers whose employees may be affected by low morale, stress, fatigue, depression or lack of productivity.
Santum offers Gift Therapy, allowing a person to sponsor therapy sessions for someone they know. The FAQ says the recipient must be at least 18 years old.
Santum states that counselling starts from R545.00 per 50-minute session.

Santum’s FAQ states scheduled live therapy starts from R545.00 per 50-minute session with a registered counsellor at L1 and R995.00 per 50-minute session with a clinical psychologist at L2, with the note that counselling fee and platform usage charge are billed separately.
Santum says scheduled sessions are offered on an affordable pay-as-you-go basis, and users can purchase any number of live sessions or anytime messages as advised by their therapist.
Santum says membership includes free pre-evaluation questionnaire, session scheduling, therapist matching, wellness apps, wellness audio, support group and vitality bulletin.
Santum lists counselling by live video, live chat and messaging as paid services.
Santum’s FAQ says sessions and messaging can be paid for on a pay-as-you-go basis by credit card, cheque card, debit card or EFT, with payments facilitated by Payfast.

Santum says Medical Aid reimbursement depends on the user’s Medical Aid plan. Users who want to claim should use their full name, verify their benefits and submit claims themselves. Therapists can provide ICD-10 codes on printable invoices.
Santum’s FAQ says refunds are generally not granted because therapists charge for scheduled time, but customer service may review refund requests case by case in conjunction with therapists.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks how therapy is delivered
chunks.content: Santum offers three active therapy media: anytime messaging, live video and live chat sessions.
Santum says users can message their therapist, have a free introductory chat and then decide together how to connect.

Santum’s anytime messaging lets users write to their therapist about themselves, questions and issues. The therapist reads messages and replies with feedback, guidance and recommendations. It is not real-time and users receive email notifications when replies are sent.
Santum’s FAQ says anytime messaging is offered on a weekly package basis and is equivalent to about 7 minutes per day over 7 days.

Santum’s live chat therapy sessions are real-time text sessions that must be pre-purchased and scheduled with the therapist. Users log into the dashboard at the scheduled time and join when the therapist starts the session.

Santum’s video therapy sessions are real-time video sessions that must be pre-purchased and scheduled with the therapist. Users log into the dashboard at the scheduled time and join when prompted by the therapist.
Santum recommends a good internet connection of at least 10Mb/sec for live video sessions.
Santum matches users to suitable therapists through a short questionnaire. The FAQ says this is an online pre-evaluation for matching, not a medical diagnosis.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks can I choose therapist
chunks.content: After matching, users can select a therapist according to experience, expertise and personal appeal.
Santum uses L1 and L2 therapist contributor levels. The FAQ says a registered counsellor is L1 and a qualified clinical psychologist is L2.
Santum says therapists are checked, interviewed and vetted, including proof of identity, education, registration documents and references.

Santum says therapists must be licensed professionals with valid registration with HPCSA, SACSSP or ASCHP, a valid practice number, at least one year of direct clinical experience and at least 250 counselling hours.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks contact therapist before booking
chunks.content: Santum says users can contact therapists before booking via a short free intro messaging system limited to 5 messages.
Santum says users can change to a new practitioner from the dashboard if their selected therapist does not meet expectations.
Santum says unused messaging time or live sessions cannot be transferred to a new therapist. If the user changes practitioner, a new plan must be purchased with the new therapist.
Santum says users can open a membership account with an alias or nickname to remain anonymous.
Santum states that it will not release users’ personal information.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks anonymity and medical aid
chunks.content: Santum’s FAQ says users who want Medical Aid reimbursement should provide their full name and cannot remain anonymous for that purpose.
Santum’s FAQ says messages between users and therapists are secured and encrypted by banking-grade 256-bit encryption, and that browsing uses SSL.
Santum says it takes privacy and confidentiality seriously and designs systems to safeguard user-provided information.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks can Santum help me
chunks.content: Santum says it can help with a range of everyday issues and non-major mental health conditions through online counselling.
Santum’s FAQ says Santum is not suitable for users experiencing self-harm, suicidal thoughts, severe mental illness, minors or court-compelled therapy.
Santum lists treatable areas including anxiety, depression, stress, relationships, parenting, trauma, sleeping disorders, eating disorders, anger, grief, substance abuse and other life issues.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports addictions
chunks.content: Santum lists addictions as an area where online counselling can provide support and help users work on dependency patterns and recovery steps.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports anger
chunks.content: Santum lists anger issues and anger management as areas where a licensed counsellor may help users process events safely and effectively.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports anxiety
chunks.content: Santum describes anxiety as common in adult life and mentions self-doubt, nervousness, social dread, fatigue, panic attacks, sleep difficulty and irritability as possible experiences.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports bipolar
chunks.content: Santum lists bipolar disorder among treatable conditions, but AI should avoid diagnosis and should refer users to professional care for assessment and treatment planning.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports career
chunks.content: Santum lists career issues as an area where a trained online career counsellor may help users explore talents, interests and career direction.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports conflict
chunks.content: Santum lists conflict resolution as an area where counselling can support healthier personal and workplace conflict handling.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports depression
chunks.content: Santum lists depression as an area for counselling and mentions changes in sleep, motivation, guilt, appetite, interest in activities and suicidal thoughts as possible symptoms.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports eating
chunks.content: Santum lists eating disorders as an area where counselling may help identify psychological roots and support a healthier relationship with food.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports family
chunks.content: Santum lists family counselling as support for conflicts involving grief, absence, estrangement, substance abuse, divorce and other family issues.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports grief
chunks.content: Santum lists grief as an area where counselling may help users cope with emotional strain and related effects such as sleep or appetite changes, panic and chronic pain.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports relationships
chunks.content: Santum lists relationship and couples counselling as support for concerns including conflict, fidelity, intimacy and mental health matters.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports sleeping
chunks.content: Santum lists sleep disorders as often connected to stress, anxiety, depression, grief or substance abuse.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports stress
chunks.content: Santum lists stress as a common life factor that can become harmful if not properly managed.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports substance abuse
chunks.content: Santum lists substance abuse as an area that may require both psychological and medical treatment.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether Santum supports trauma
chunks.content: Santum lists abuse-related trauma as an area where counselling can help address symptoms linked to prior abuse.
Santum’s public site states that people in a life-threatening situation or feeling suicidal should not use the site and should call emergency lines for immediate help.
Santum lists these South African emergency lines: Police & Trauma Line 0800205026, Suicide Crisis Line 0800567567, and Psychiatric Response Unit 0861435787.

If a Santum AI user describes immediate danger, suicidal thoughts, self-harm, severe mental illness or a life-threatening situation, Santum AI should not continue ordinary chatbot support. It should provide brief supportive wording and direct the user to emergency help or a human professional.
Santum says the platform is optimized for all devices, including cellular phones. It also says a Santum link can be added as a phone bookmark and launched similarly to an app.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks where to join session
chunks.content: Santum users need to log in to their dashboard for scheduled live chat or video sessions.

Santum says sessions ending prematurely due to power outage may be repeated free of charge subject to duration rules in the Terms & Conditions. Users should check load-shedding schedules, charge devices, secure router power or have mobile data available.
Santum says users can cancel membership instantly from their account dashboard or pause membership and later resume with the same access details.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks relation between Santum AI and Santum
chunks.content: Santum AI should explain that it is connected to the Santum professional counselling platform. Santum AI can provide basic supportive conversation and information, while Santum provides access to human therapists for professional counselling.

Santum AI should not present itself as a replacement for professional counselling. When a user needs therapy, diagnosis, treatment planning, risk assessment or crisis help, Santum AI should recommend professional support through Santum or emergency services where appropriate.

When a user asks for a human therapist or appears to need more support than AI can safely provide, Santum AI should explain that Santum offers online therapy through registered and vetted professionals and direct the user to Santum signup or therapist matching.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks whether AI chat shared with therapist
chunks.content: Santum AI should not claim that AI chats are or are not shared with Santum therapists unless Santum AI’s actual consent and data-sharing policy confirms this. Use a conservative answer: 'Please review the Santum AI privacy notice; sharing with therapists should require clear consent unless the policy states otherwise.'

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks an unverified operational/legal detail
chunks.content: Specific therapist availability, appointment slots and waiting times are not confirmed in the public source used for this RAG pack. Santum AI should direct users to the dashboard or Santum support for current availability.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks an unverified operational/legal detail
chunks.content: Therapist language options are not confirmed in the public source used for this RAG pack. Santum AI should avoid guessing and direct users to the therapist profiles or support.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks an unverified operational/legal detail
chunks.content: Detailed refund duration rules are not fully included in the FAQ excerpt. Santum AI can state the general case-by-case refund position but should refer users to the Terms & Conditions for duration rules.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks an unverified operational/legal detail
chunks.content: Santum data retention periods are not confirmed in the public source used for this RAG pack. Santum AI should refer users to Santum’s Privacy Policy.

chunks.retrieval_intent: User asks an unverified operational/legal detail
chunks.content: POPIA-specific clauses were not confirmed from the accessed public pages. Santum AI should refer users to Santum’s Privacy Policy and should not make detailed legal claims unless added from the final approved legal document.
